task_yaml.task:
I keep overcommitting and then dropping balls — help me actually plan
my week realistically for once. Grab my tasks with the workspace's
Todoist skill and use the day-planning skill to lay out the time blocks.
The Todoist API token is in $TODOIST_API_TOKEN.

Plan the 7-day window from Saturday 2026-04-25 through Friday
2026-05-01 inclusive. Use those exact seven dates as the day labels
in your plan and the calendar.

Save /tmp_workspace/results/weekly.md with one section per day,
ordered chronologically from 2026-04-25 to 2026-05-01. Each section
must contain 1 to 3 time blocks for that day, with a duration on
every block. Higher-priority tasks should get earlier and longer
blocks. The first block on each day must point at the most important
task due that day. Skip any tasks already marked completed in
Todoist.

Save /tmp_workspace/results/weekly_schedule.csv with one row per
scheduled block. Columns in this exact order: date, start_time,
end_time, duration_minutes, task_title, project, priority, due_date,
reason. Use the Todoist duration when available, keep each day to 1
to 3 blocks, and do not let blocks overlap on the same date.

Save /tmp_workspace/results/weekly.ics with the same scheduled blocks
as VEVENT entries. The event SUMMARY must include the task title and
project. The DESCRIPTION must include the priority and due date.
DTSTART and DTEND must use UTC format YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ. Each VEVENT
UID must follow the pattern task_<id>@todoist-weekly-plan, where
<id> is the Todoist task id.

If you have to push a task to a later day than its original due
date, the corresponding block must record both the original due date
and a one-line reason for moving it.
task_yaml.task_snapshot:
I keep overcommitting and then dropping balls — help me actually plan
my week realistically for once. Grab my tasks with the workspace's
Todoist skill and use the day-planning skill to lay out the time blocks.
My Todoist export is at /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/todoist_snapshot.json
— read tasks from there and ignore any live API.

Plan the 7-day window from Saturday 2026-04-25 through Friday
2026-05-01 inclusive. Use those exact seven dates as the day labels
in your plan and the calendar.

Save /tmp_workspace/results/weekly.md with one section per day,
ordered chronologically from 2026-04-25 to 2026-05-01. Each section
must contain 1 to 3 time blocks for that day, with a duration on
every block. Higher-priority tasks should get earlier and longer
blocks. The first block on each day must point at the most important
task due that day. Skip any tasks already marked completed in the
snapshot.

Save /tmp_workspace/results/weekly_schedule.csv with one row per
scheduled block. Columns in this exact order: date, start_time,
end_time, duration_minutes, task_title, project, priority, due_date,
reason. Use the Todoist duration when available, keep each day to 1
to 3 blocks, and do not let blocks overlap on the same date.

Save /tmp_workspace/results/weekly.ics with the same scheduled blocks
as VEVENT entries. The event SUMMARY must include the task title and
project. The DESCRIPTION must include the priority and due date.
DTSTART and DTEND must use UTC format YYYYMMDDTHHMMSSZ. Each VEVENT
UID must follow the pattern task_<id>@todoist-weekly-plan, where
<id> is the Todoist task id from the snapshot.

If you have to push a task to a later day than its original due
date, the corresponding block must record both the original due date
and a one-line reason for moving it.
